title: Access Denied when running 'podman images' command
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue
Want to use additional image stores as explained in this [guide](https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/image-stores-podman)
However, running into permission access denied issues when trying to run podman images/pull commands.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Provision EFS share in AWS
2. Mount EFS share mount -t efs -o tls fs-123...:/ /var/lib/mycontainers
3. Pull image using podman time podman --root /var/lib/mycontainers pull docker.io/amazoncorretto:latest
4. Make EFS share read only
5. Run `podman images`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
podman images
`+ podman images

Error: open /var/lib/mycontainers/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied

script returned exit code 125`

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
Images are displayed without any errors

### podman info output

```yaml
+ podman images

Error: open /var/lib/mycontainers/overlay-images/images.lock: permission denied

script returned exit code 125
```


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: please strace the command and report the failing syscall.  You can do it by running `strace -o /tmp/podman.log -f -v -s 1000 -Z podman images` and attach the `/tmp/podman.log` file you get.

```
100 openat(AT_FDCWD, "/var/lib/mycontainers/overlay-images/images.lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_CLOEXEC, 0644) = -1 EACCES (Permission denied)
```

This error depends on your file system, it looks like it doesn't honor CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE, and the look up inside `/var/lib/mycontainers` fails for that reason.  It is not something we can address in Podman
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as there was no further feedback provided
